title: Machine learning based accident assessment
why: Allstate's primary ML claim-assessment patent. Defines the decision pipeline they apply to incoming claims.

title: Commercial claim processing platform using machine learning
why: ML claims processing platform — describes their training/inference pipeline.

title: Mobile claims assessment
why: Allstate's automated mobile photo damage assessment without human adjuster involvement — first wave.

title: Property image generation and assessment
why: Captures the photo-evaluation logic Allstate's AI applies to inbound damage photos.

title: Processing systems and methods having a machine learning engine for providing a surface dimension output
why: Allstate's roof-measurement AI — automated dimension calculation. Drives their square-foot scope before adjuster ever arrives.

title: Data processing system with machine learning engine to provide output generation functions
why: General ML output-generation framework Allstate uses to auto-generate claim outcomes.

title: Method and system for automated roof estimating
why: THE foundational carrier-vendor patent for automated roof estimating with per-carrier guideline application. Shingle severity, repair-vs-replace, steep charges, ice/water.

title: Method and system for automated roof estimating - extension
why: Extension of the Accurence roof estimating patent — refines per-carrier ruleset.

title: Method and system for automated roof estimating - imagery rules
why: Image-analysis rules layer — what visual features the system looks for in aerial imagery.

title: System and method for ScopeAssist scope automation
why: ScopeAssist patent — how the carrier-side scope is auto-generated.

title: Systems and methods for performing image analysis
why: Accurence's image analysis layer — applies carrier-specific rules to images, generates settlements without physical inspection.

title: Computer-implemented methods for analyzing real property damage claims
why: State Farm's property damage claim analysis system. Cited in some lawsuit filings.

title: Methods and systems for capturing real property data and a building plan structure
why: State Farm's property/structure capture system — feeds their automated damage assessment.

title: Method and system for evaluating insurance claim with hazard imagery
why: Evaluates claims using hazard imagery (storm/hail). Likely covers their hail-evaluation logic.

title: Storm event roof damage detection
why: Direct hit — roof damage detection after storm events. Core to their hail-payout algorithm.

title: Systems and Methods for Automating Property Assessment Using Probable Roof Loss Confidence Scores
why: State Farm's confidence-score system: building data + roof data + historical hail + climate region = automated underwriting/claims decisions. THIS is the engine that decides if your claim ever sees a human.

title: Systems and methods of utilizing unmanned vehicles to detect insurance claim buildup
why: State Farm's drone-based fraud detection — looks for 'claim buildup' (allegedly inflated claims). Roofers must understand what triggers this so legitimate scopes don't get flagged.

title: Systems and methods for generating insurance claims based on aerial imagery analysis
why: USAA's generative AI for aerial imagery — analyzes before/after, initiates claims.

title: Systems and methods for detecting items at a property
why: USAA's property-item detector — what's on/around the property. Used for both underwriting and post-loss validation.

title: Machine learning-based recognition of weather damage
why: Classifier distinguishes hail / wind / ice / wear-and-tear / human-made damage. Lays out the EXACT visual signatures the algorithm uses for each damage type — this is the wear-and-tear definition you're up against.

title: Property investigation system and method
why: Betterview is used by 100+ carriers for property risk scoring. Generates wear-and-tear scores from satellite/aerial — frequently the basis for pre-loss non-renewal or post-loss reduction.

title: Image recognition system for roof damage detection and management
why: UAV-based roof imagery + 3D structural model + damage feature extraction. Used by carriers as third-party adjuster verification.

title: Technologies for using image data analysis to assess and classify hail damage
why: Travelers' CNN hail damage classifier. Convolutional neural network segments roof images, extracts features, outputs confidence score on whether damage is hail-caused. This is the primary algorithm behind Travelers' hail denial decisions.

title: Machine learning-based image recognition of weather damage
why: Travelers' own ML weather damage recognition patent — US national filing. Defines the training data, feature extraction, and damage-type classification pipeline for weather events including hail and wind.

title: Intelligent system and method for assessing structural damage using aerial imagery
why: Travelers' most recent aerial damage assessment patent (2024). Uses vertical aerial imagery for straight-down property views — their drone program's core AI. Defines the decision pipeline that produces the scope adjuster confronts the homeowner with.

title: Drones with sensors used in insurance applications
why: Liberty Mutual's own drone inspection patent — sensors collect feedback data, drone autonomously repositions based on detected damage signals. This defines how LM conducts physical inspections to deny or reduce claims.

title: Method of modeling roof age of a structure
why: Models 'real' roof age from tree height, structures, weather. Carriers use this to dispute homeowner-reported roof age — and to reduce ACV depreciation arguments.
